item.title: Amazon’s ambition to rival Starlink set back after Blue Origin rocket grounded
item.description: Federal Aviation Administration forces Jeff Bezos’s space venture to halt operations of flagship New Glenn

item.title: Anthropic and Amazon agree $100bn AI infrastructure deal
item.description: Start-up behind Claude tool seeks to bulk up on chips and computing power after suffering outages this year

item.title: FirstFT: Apple CEO Tim Cook will step down in September
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: China-Japan tensions grow, and Keir Starmer fends off calls to quit

item.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to hand over to John Ternus in September
item.description: Head of hardware to become Apple’s next chief executive while Cook will become iPhone maker’s chair

item.title: The M&A strategy behind Berkshire Hathaway’s tie-up with Tokio Marine
item.description: Deal with non-life insurer is first investment in Japan beyond trading houses for Warren Buffett’s conglomerate

item.title: What does AI really mean for your work? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Sarah O’Connor, John Burn Murdoch and Madhumita Murgia on Thursday April 23 at 1pm BST

item.title: Trump’s call to deregulate psychedelics boosts drugmakers’ shares
item.description: Stock in UK-based Compass Pathways jumps to highest level in two years following president’s executive order

item.title: US Export-Import Bank boosts energy lending during Iran conflict
item.description: Trade finance agency ‘inundated’ with requests to support US oil and gas exports, says president John Jovanovic

item.title: William Hill’s owner can squeeze extra winnings from its suitor
item.description: A bid for owner Evoke sounds generous, but cost cuts might boost returns further

item.title: Brad Jacobs’ QXO takes a high-speed approach to permanent capital
item.description: Billionaire investor’s acquisition vehicle has struck its third major building products deal in a year, spending $30bn in total

item.title: US oil refiners reap windfall from Iran war
item.description: Soaring fuel prices and access to cheap North American crude oil put American refiners in pole position

item.title: Revamping EU merger rules is not a cure-all
item.description: A bigger priority is completing the single market and streamlining corporate law

item.title: America’s coming revolt is in the ‘wired belt’
item.description: Anger in the rust belt propelled Trump to victory — the rage of suburban knowledge workers will drive the AI backlash

item.title: Data centre hopeful Fermi’s shares plunge as top executives quit
item.description: Company co-founded by former Trump energy secretary beset by setbacks including loss of $150mn Amazon investment

item.title: Heathrow ticket prices can rise to pay for third runway bid, regulator says
item.description: Civil Aviation Authority’s draft decision comes despite criticism from carriers over airport’s expansion project

item.title: Washington-backed rare earth group to buy Brazilian miner for $2.8bn
item.description: USA Rare Earth’s deal for Serra Verde highlights America’s push to secure access to critical minerals

item.title: ENRC sues for more than $168mn over botched SFO investigation
item.description: Kazakh miner seeks compensation after UK fraud agency’s probe that closed in 2023

item.title: Miliband unveils move to delink gas and electricity prices
item.description: UK energy secretary’s push comes as Iran war threatens Labour pledge to cut heating bills

item.title: The trade resilience that’s doing Trump a favour
item.description: Absence of freight shipping crisis gives US time to pursue stand-off with Iran

item.title: Netflix’s Reed Hastings: an icon of good leadership and bad governance
item.description: Streamer’s co-founder steps away as his progeny faces its biggest challenge

item.title: Five techy wedding gifts that go beyond the registry
item.description: Fun gadgets for the modern married couple

item.title: UniCredit steps up pursuit of Commerzbank with new plan
item.description: Andrea Orcel says German lender risks becoming ‘increasingly unfit’ for rapidly changing banking market

item.title: Kevin Warsh: Trump’s next fall guy at the Fed?
item.description: The nominee to lead the world’s most powerful central bank wants big changes. But there’s risk of confrontation with the president over interest rates

item.title: William Hill owner in takeover talks with gaming operator Bally’s Intralot
item.description: Evoke in talks to sell itself after taking a hit from UK tax rises on gambling

item.title: Yes, tokens do have real uses in finance
item.description: Tokenisation is taking hold in areas including money, bonds, gold, funds, real estate and private equity

item.title: US stablecoins pose danger to emerging markets, say central bankers
item.description: Growth of dollar-pegged crypto assets under Donald Trump risks accelerating dollarisation and enabling criminal misuse

item.title: Wealth advisers made billions from private capital fees
item.description: Plus, RIP Mark Mobius, BlackRock cools on Europe and Jasper Johns in Bilbao

item.title: Private credit gets the once-over
item.description: Do you want the bad news or the bad news?

item.title: FirstFT: Starmer fights for his future
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: US seizes Iranian ship and Sweden says Russian economy is faltering

item.title: Risk Management: Financial Institutions
item.description: The soaring level of US government debt; how AI is increasing the speed and depth of cyber attacks; and geopolitical shocks have highlighted the need for diversity in cloud providers
